Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(9 comments)

Adding Qian, since he did work related to this but on the server side.  

Qian, I'm not sure if you still have the RPC simulator you used for your paper, but that would be very useful for more easily developing the best algorithm for tuning the clients.

One benefit of handling this on the client means there is no need for a protocol change, and the clients can tune differently depending on their own situation (e.g. WAN client differently than local client).

Jinshan, it would be good to get an indication in the commit comment if this is just a patch you are looking for feedback on, how much you've tested it, etc.  Otherwise, it is possible it will get some +1 reviews and land, but is just a test patch and kills performance or similar.

Patch Set 1:

Hi, the lustre simulator is available at https://github.com/yingjinqian/Lustre-Simulator. I used it for simuating some TBF algorithms. It already implemented All AT algo including early reply algo. I will investigate JinShan's algo  of flow contorl for OSC when I have time.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

I think it would be good to make a few more changes to explain the flow control better.  Ideally, also a slight improvement to add some hysteresis to the change so that it doesn't change rapidly under flow control. 

We would also need to have some testing on the patch to demonstrate that it is doing the right thing under load. A basic sanity test that adds request processing delay would be good, to verify that the RPCs in flight is tuned.

Also, will you be able to test this at your site to verify it is solving your overload problems?  It doesn't really make sense to land it and wait until 2.12 is released, only to find that the algorithm is not working well under load. Getting feedback control algorithms to work well can be difficult. 

Qian, would you have time to run this through your simulator to see if it works well?

Patch Set 2:

parents.subject: LU-11009 test: add version check to test_102

commit.subject: LU-11189 osc: flow control for OSC
commit.message:
LU-11189 osc: flow control for OSC

Add a simple flow control algorithm to OSC device.
In this algorithm, OSC will shrink RPC window size once it receives
early reply messages, which indicates the server is already in
congestion. Shrinking window size will hold sending even more
RPCs to server to make things worse.

If the OSC is not in flow control mode, it will slowly enlarge the RPC
window up to max_rpcs_in_flight.

This alogrithm has no effect when the load on server is light.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@uber.com>
Change-Id: Idea117a3b8466155581c3c052ec7dcff491bfff3
